title: Error: OCI runtime error: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: error adding seccomp filter rule for syscall bdflush: requested action matches default action of filter
body:
I get the error "Error: OCI runtime error: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: error adding seccomp filter rule for syscall bdflush: requested action matches default action of filter" upon trying to run any new containers.

Amusingly enough, I have running containers managed by systemctl and they seem fine.

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I have podman installed as described in the [official documentation](https://podman.io/getting-started/installation). My exact yum deplist is below including runc. I am running CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009 (Core).

I ran a yum update and moved from 

```
Sep 01 09:04:39 Updated: 4:containers-common-1-18.el7.20.1.noarch (this version worked)
Dec 01 11:13:11 Updated: 4:containers-common-1-18.el7.22.1.noarch (this version breaks)
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```sh
[root@thinkcentre ~]# cat /etc/centos-release
CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009 (Core)
[root@thinkcentre ~]# uname -a
Linux thinkcentre.localdomain 3.10.0-1160.49.1.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Tue Nov 30 15:51:32 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
[root@thinkcentre ~]# podman run busybox
Error: OCI runtime error: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: error adding seccomp filter rule for syscall bdflush: requested action matches default action of filter
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Built:        Mon Mar  1 15:30:40 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes. I have updated all packages in the CentOS version via the yum package manager and am unable to run podman.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Phyical machine

comments.body:
This is not an upstream issue, you should open a bugzilla if you want support.

The problem is likely a RHEL7 box does not understand one of the seccomp syscalls.
You probably need to remove bdflush from the seccomp.json file.

@rhatdan thx for your gracious help, I did indeed try removing `bdflush` from `/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json` and it seems to then jump to the syscall element in this array, failing with the same error but now `kexec_file_load`.

Could you help me understand what the implications of the syscalls in this array are, or point me to an appropriate resource?

Would you kindly point me to the Bugzilla so I can request support there!

Many thanks!

Basically these are syscalls that are supported in newer kernels, and not supported in RHEL7 kernels.
The tooling uses a library which you did not  update (And probably should not, libseccomp) which can not translate the string names to a syscall, so it is rejecting them.  Removing them is save since they are not available on RHEL7.

I would just go through them one by one and remove.  Or just get the old version os seccomp.json and just use that.

@rhatdan cool thanks a lot for the information! I will try removing them.

I am a bit confused why the RHEL7 kernels do not receive updates including to the kernel, as CentOS's official EOL for 7.9.2009 is Jun 30th, 2024. I guess maintenance means only critical patches in this context.
This is not a kernel update but a library update libseccomp. And kernel updates do not include syscall changes, just back ports.
seccomp.conf from Tag v0/39.0 is the last one that worked for me (libseccomp-2.3.1-4.el7)
